mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: High real estate sales combined with elevated policy rates create a financial stress channel where housing sector deleveraging transmits to broader equity markets, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and RSAFS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops of > 1% fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds the 310 index threshold, markets price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy and margin erosion, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 310.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, breaking the inflation-aversion link.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment signal a synchronized economic expansion that boosts consumer confidence and equity demand, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST/UNRATE signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no joint predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP revisions that exceed prior estimates confirm underlying economic resilience, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP revisions are followed by negative 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, confirming a decoupling between growth data and equity valuations.
